messages.content:
Which of the following statements is correct?

a.
The prefrontal cortex is relatively late to develop.

b.
The sensory cortex is relatively late to develop.

c.
The visual cortex is relatively late to develop.

d.
The motor cortex is relatively late to develop
a. The prefrontal cortex is relatively late to develop.

Which of the following statements is correct?

a.
Neural pathways in the occipital and temporal lobes develop early in life.

b.
The brain grows in the same way for all infants up to the age of five.

c.
The parietal, frontal and cerebellum lobes are the first to mature.

d.
The pre-frontal cortex is the very first part of the brain to mature
c. The parietal, frontal, and cerebellum lobes are the first to mature.

Which of the following statements is NOT correct?

a.
Physiologically, the cerebral cortex is where learning is achieved.

b.
Grey matter is a term for the convoluted outer layer of the brain.

c.
Learning is the process of the brain producing new brain cells.

d.
Higher-order executive functions occur in the prefrontal cortex
c. Learning is the process of the brain producing new brain cells.

Which statement is NOT correct?

a.
Major functions of the frontal lobe include voluntary motor and executive functions.

b.
Major functions of the frontal lobe include decision-making, planning and judgement.

c.
Major functions of the frontal lobe include understanding language and forming new memories.

d.
Major functions of the frontal lobe include thinking about possible consequences
c. Major functions of the frontal lobe include understanding language and forming new memories.

a.
have no increased risk of depression, generalised anxiety disorder, or substance abuse

b.
perform normally on tasks of memory and executive function after effective counselling

c.
have higher than normal levels of glucocorticoid cortisol and associated neurobiology

d.
have increased hippocampal and prefrontal cortex volume, which causes behavioural issues
d. have increased hippocampal and prefrontal cortex volume, which causes behavioral issues

Trauma affected youth have been found to:

a.
have no increased risk of depression, generalised anxiety disorder, or substance abuse

b.
perform normally on tasks of memory and executive function after effective counselling

c.
have higher than normal levels of glucocorticoid cortisol and associated neurobiology

d.
have increased hippocampal and prefrontal cortex volume, which causes behavioural issues
c. have higher than normal levels of glucocorticoid cortisol and associated neurobiology

Students who have experienced trauma are likely to exhibit which of the following?

a.
generally unaffected memory, executive thinking, and relationship building skills

b.
some specific learning difficulties, but mostly unaffected attention and concentration

c.
difficulties with forming relationships, limited attention span, and memory difficulties

d.
difficulties processing language, but intact executive skills (e.g., decision making)
c. difficulties with forming relationships, limited attention span, and memory difficulties

Common outcomes of trauma do NOT include:

a.
low self-esteem and aggression

b.
general irritability and anxiety

c.
accelerated socio-emotional development

d.
difficulties with processing and expressing emotions

Which of the following statements is correct?

a.
The prefrontal cortex is relatively late to develop.

b.
The sensory cortex is relatively late to develop.

c.
The visual cortex is relatively late to develop.

d.
The motor cortex is relatively late to develop
a. The prefrontal cortex is relatively late to develop.
